description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring two large, rectangular solar panel arrays mounted on a flat, platform-like base. The panels are elevated and tilted, supported by visible structural frames, and are positioned at opposite ends of the base. The base itself is a broad, rectangular slab with a mix of smooth and textured surfaces, and includes several smaller geometric structures, possibly representing electrical housings, vents, or access points. The color palette is dominated by shades of black, gray, blue, and some red accents, suggesting a modern, industrial design. The panels appear to be made of glass or photovoltaic material, while the supporting structures are likely metal, and the base may be concrete or composite. The overall form and layout suggest this installation is intended for urban environments, such as rooftops or dedicated solar farms.
description: A modern solar energy installation with two large, tilted solar panel arrays on a broad, rectangular base featuring various geometric structures and a mix of black, gray, blue, and red colors.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two tilted solar panels, one closer and one further back, with the base and several smaller structures visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angles of both solar panels and the elongated rectangular base with its surface details.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the solar panels and the red-accented edge of the base, with smaller structures more prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing both solar panels from the other side, with the base's width and surface features clearly visible.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly defined, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with metal (likely aluminum or steel) support frames
attribute_array_configuration: two separate arrays, each consisting of a single large panel, positioned at opposite ends of the base
